description: Add missing configSchema property to the Plugin Class SDK reference page.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Add configSchema to Plugin Class optional properties table
userStories.description: Add the configSchema property to the optional properties table and class signature in the Plugin Class SDK reference, with usage example.
acceptanceCriteria: Class signature code block includes 'readonly configSchema?: ConfigSchema;' line
Optional properties table includes configSchema row with type ConfigSchema, description mentioning declaring settings that users provide via config.json or the side panel
A brief example shows how to declare a configSchema with a url-type field
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/sdk/plugin-class.mdx (add configSchema to class signature, optional properties table, and example)

Context:
The OpenTabsPlugin base class (platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts, line 101) declares:
`readonly configSchema?: ConfigSchema;`
The ConfigSchema type is imported from @opentabs-dev/shared. The Plugin Class reference page currently lists excludePatterns and homepage as optional properties but omits configSchema.

Changes:
1. In the class signature code block (around line 18-36), add `readonly configSchema?: ConfigSchema;` after `readonly homepage?: string;`
2. In the Optional Properties table (around line 82-104), add a new row:
   - configSchema | ConfigSchema | Typed configuration schema — declares settings fields that users provide via config.json or the side panel. Fields of type 'url' can derive Chrome match patterns at runtime. See Configuration reference for details.
3. After the homepage example block (around line 124), add a configSchema example:
   ```typescript
   import type { ConfigSchema } from '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk';

   class MyPlugin extends OpenTabsPlugin {
     readonly configSchema: ConfigSchema = {
       instanceUrl: { type: 'url', label: 'Instance URL', required: true },
     };
     // ...
   }
   ```

Constraints:
- Import the ConfigSchema type in the example (it's re-exported from @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk).
- Do NOT add extensive documentation about the settings system here — link to the Configuration reference page instead.
- Match the existing table formatting pattern.
